skill.name: super-swarm-spark
skill.description: Coordinates a rolling pool of Sparky subagents to execute development plan tasks in parallel, validate completions, and reconcile integration work.
skill.summary: Run plan-based development tasks through a controlled parallel subagent workflow.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown code formatting and ordinary prose in SKILL.md. No malicious code, network exfiltration, credential access, or prompt injection attempt was found. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it directs many subagents to edit files, run validation, update plans, and commit changes.

medium_findings.title: Parallel File Modification and Commit Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs subagents to implement changes, update plan files, run validation, and commit their own work. This is legitimate for a coding orchestration skill, but it can cause broad filesystem changes and repository history changes if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require file edits, validation, plan updates, and commits. This confirms operational risk, but the context is a development workflow rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: High-Concurrency Subagent Orchestration
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the orchestrator to keep up to 12 subagents active and to ignore dependency maps. This can introduce race conditions, conflicting edits, duplicated work, or incomplete integration review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The concurrency limit and instruction to ignore dependency maps are explicit. The risk depends on project complexity and user oversight, so confidence is high for risk presence but not for harmful outcome.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives from Markdown
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution findings are Markdown inline code or fenced examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The reported weak cryptography findings are ordinary text matches in descriptions, task metadata, or placeholders, not cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation, task prompt templates, or Markdown code fences. There is no executable script file or Ruby code in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance location requires the subagent role value to be sparky. It does not collect host, network, process, environment, or identity information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a role constraint, not a command or instruction to inspect the system. No reconnaissance behavior is present at that location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Delegated Validation and Git Commit Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells each subagent to run validation when feasible and commit completed work. Users should run it only in repositories where automated edits and local commits are acceptable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions are explicit and operationally sensitive. They do not show malicious intent, but they can alter repository state.

content.value_statement: Large development plans are slow when tasks are processed one at a time. This skill coordinates parallel subagents, validates results, and performs an integration pass.
actual_capabilities: Parses markdown plan files into task units and optional task subsets.
Builds task context packs with canonical file paths and naming constraints.
Runs a rolling pool of up to 12 Sparky subagents.
Validates subagent completions against expected task outcomes.
Coordinates final integration fixes, tests, and plan status updates.
limitations: Requires a clear markdown plan with identifiable task sections.
Depends on the availability of a Sparky subagent role in the host tool.
Can create conflicting edits when plan dependencies are incomplete.
Requires human review before publishing or merging generated commits.

use_cases.title: Execute a Large Refactor Plan
use_cases.description: Split a prepared refactor plan into parallel implementation tasks with shared path constraints.

use_cases.title: Accelerate Feature Delivery
use_cases.description: Run independent feature tasks at the same time while tracking completion in the plan file.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Test and Integration Work
use_cases.description: Assign validation, fix follow-up issues, and produce an execution summary after implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use super-swarm-spark to execute ./plans/feature-plan.md from start to finish. Keep the plan file updated and summarize validation results.

prompt_templates.title: Run Selected Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use super-swarm-spark with ./plans/auth-plan.md for tasks T1, T2, and T4 only. Enforce the canonical paths from the plan.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Conflict Control
prompt_templates.prompt: Use super-swarm-spark for ./plans/refactor-plan.md. Before launching work, identify shared files and include strict path guidance in each task context.

prompt_templates.title: Complete and Integrate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use super-swarm-spark to finish ./plans/migration-plan.md, validate each completion, resolve integration conflicts, and report test status.

output_examples.input: Run the full profile plan with super-swarm-spark.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports assigned tasks, completed tasks, integration fixes, validation status, and files changed.

output_examples.input: Run only tasks T3 and T7 from the billing plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the selected tasks, confirms canonical file paths, and summarizes each task result.

output_examples.input: Finish the migration plan and reconcile conflicts.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides a concise execution summary with completed work, blockers, tests, and next steps.

best_practices: Use this skill only with reviewed plan files and clear task boundaries.
Run it in a clean branch so automated commits are easy to inspect.
Review all subagent changes and validation output before merging.
anti_patterns: Do not use it on vague plans without explicit file paths.
Do not run it in repositories with sensitive uncommitted changes.
Do not treat parallel subagent output as a substitute for code review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It coordinates parallel subagents to execute tasks from a markdown development plan.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: It delegates implementation work to subagents, so repository files may be changed.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: It instructs subagents to run validation when feasible and to commit completed work.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it can modify files and repository history.

faq.question: What plan format does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects markdown task sections such as T1 or Task 1.1 with locations and acceptance criteria.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: It is best for experienced developers coordinating large, well-scoped coding plans.
